title: Speaking: Asking About Objects
learningGoal: Perform a conversation asking what various objects are, confirming their identity, and answering questions.
instructions: You are at a local market with a friend. Point out items and ask what they are. Also, answer your friend's questions. Remember: 'Yeh' for things near you, 'Woh' for things far away.
speakingPrompt: Respond to the audio prompts using full Hindi sentences.
scenario: Walking through a market and identifying objects.

subTasks.prompt: Point to something close to you and ask Ravi: 'What is this?'
Ravi answers: 'Yeh kitaab hai.' (This is a book.) Now point to something far away and ask: 'And what is that?'
Ravi answers: 'Woh gaadi hai.' (That is a car.) Now Ravi points to an object near you and asks: 'Kya yeh kampyutar hai?' (Is this a computer?). Answer him: 'Yes, this is a computer.'
You are thirsty. Say: 'This is water.'

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Ji haan' instead of 'Haan' is also perfectly fine.

criteria.name: Demonstrative Pronoun Accuracy
criteria.description: Uses 'Yeh' for the first and third prompts, and 'Woh' for the second.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Yeh kya hai? Aur woh kya hai? Haan, yeh kampyutar hai. Yeh paani hai.

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! You can now navigate simple environments and ask what things are.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review the difference between 'Yeh' (this) and 'Woh' (that). Also remember that 'kya' means 'what'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Try to link the words together smoothly: 'Yeh kya_hai' instead of pausing between each word.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: यह क्या है? और वह क्या है? हाँ, यह कंप्यूटर है। यह पानी है।

hintText: Speak with a rising intonation when asking 'Yeh kya hai?' to indicate a question.
commonMistakes: Answering 'Haan, kya yeh kampyutar hai' instead of 'Haan, yeh kampyutar hai' (leaving the question word in the answer).
